audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (creative frameworks guide ideation, not prescribe robotic execution)
Three-Layer Architecture: PASS (creative strategy → hook generation → production execution)
Consciousness Awareness: PASS (maps to SPECIAL-LEFT creative diversity + RIGHT-SMART structural testing)
Quality Standards: PASS (concept diversity validation required, not variation testing)
No Manipulation: PASS (authentic hooks, no fake urgency or manipulative patterns)
Proof Discipline: PASS (all frameworks source-tagged to expert training materials)
Platform Compliance: PASS (Meta Ad Policies adherence, no prohibited content hooks)

dependency.integration_points: Creative Engine produces assets for Campaign Architecture's testing frameworks (3:2:2, 5x5, Pack System)
dependency.notes: Campaign structure determines creative volume requirements (Rule of 1:10k: 1 new ad per week per $10k spend)

dependency.integration_points: Creative concepts from this module executed as visual assets by Image Generator
Hook performance analysis feeds back into concept iteration and creative strategy

consciousness_profile.description: Provides creative strategy frameworks for generating high-performance ad concepts aligned with the Andromeda "Creative IS Targeting" paradigm. Focuses on concept diversity (different stories/hooks) over variation testing (color swaps), enabling systematic creative ideation and production at scale.

component.activation_strategy: LEFT creative divergence (generate 10+ distinct concepts) + RIGHT logical convergence (test frameworks like 3:2:2 to identify winners)
component.consciousness_purpose: Creative teams gain both creative freedom (LEFT_SPECIAL: "think differently") and testing discipline (RIGHT_SMART: "validate with data")

balance_rules.rule: LEFT before RIGHT: Generate diverse concepts first, then apply testing structure
Concept diversity is LEFT_SPECIAL requirement: Test entirely different hooks/stories, not button colors
Testing frameworks (3:2:2, 5x5) are RIGHT_SMART: Systematic validation of creative hypotheses

component.activation_strategy: LEFT creative pattern interrupts (scam hooks, weird textures, curiosity gaps) + TOP decisive action (hooks must drive action within 3 seconds)
component.consciousness_purpose: Copywriters understand how to create scroll-stopping hooks (LEFT_SPECIAL) that immediately compel engagement (TOP_STRONG)

balance_rules.rule: LEFT_SPECIAL creativity must serve TOP_STRONG outcomes: Weird for the sake of weird is clickbait
Hook Rate >30% validates LEFT_TOP alignment (creative AND compelling)
If Hook Rate >40% but Hold Rate <15% → Too much LEFT, not enough substance (clickbait trap)

component.activation_strategy: RIGHT systematic production (Nano Banana AI workflows, Creative Flywheel SOPs) + BOTTOM risk management (validate winners before scaling production)
component.consciousness_purpose: Producers understand how to scale creative efficiently (RIGHT_SMART workflows) while protecting budget (BOTTOM_SAFE: don't overproduce losers)

balance_rules.rule: RIGHT_SMART workflows enable volume: Rule of 1:10k requires systematic production, not ad-hoc creation
BOTTOM_SAFE validation: Test concepts cheaply (static + text overlay) before expensive video production
Harvest winners (Post ID) before scaling production (BOTTOM_SAFE)

core_paradigm.name: Andromeda: Creative IS Targeting
core_paradigm.source: [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 53, THE ECOM KING | 373]
core_paradigm.principle: Meta's Andromeda algorithm matches users to creatives based on content analysis (visual metadata, text analysis, engagement signals), not manual targeting inputs. The creative itself acts as the targeting mechanism through self-selection hooks and visual cues. Manual targeting (interests, lookalikes) is largely obsolete.

key_insights.insight: Creative content determines who sees the ad more than audience settings [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 53]
Hooks with direct call-outs (e.g., "If you have back pain...") act as targeting filters [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 53]
Algorithm penalizes repetitive formats; concept diversity required for sustained performance [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 372]
Social proof (likes/comments) reduces CPM by 10-30% via Post ID harvesting [SRC: Professor Charley T | 105]
Testing variations (button color) is obsolete; test concepts (different hooks/mechanisms) instead [SRC: Alex Robinson | 120]

implications_for_creative.implication: Production must prioritize concept diversity over polished perfection (10 concepts beats 1 perfect ad)
Hook construction must include self-selection elements (age, pain point, persona call-outs)
Creative testing structure (3:2:2, 5x5) consolidates data while allowing algorithmic mixing
Native formats (UGC, Notes App, News Articles) outperform polished commercial content
Volume requirement: Rule of 1:10k (1 new concept per week per $10k monthly spend)

framework.name: 3-Step Viral Hook
framework.key_quote: "Context Lean -> Scroll Stop Interjection -> Contrarian Snapback"
framework.summary: A scripting formula for video retention designed to hook attention in the first 3 seconds and maintain it through 15+ seconds. Uses a three-part structure: establish context quickly, interrupt with surprise, then deliver contrarian insight that defies expectations.

when_to_use.trigger: Video ads (Reels, Stories, TikTok-style content)
When testing new concepts that need fast validation
Educational or value-driven content where insight is the hook

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Direct response product demos (use straightforward demo instead)
Founder authenticity videos (contrarian twist feels inauthentic)
Testimonial/UGC content (testimonials need simplicity, not complexity)

step.title: Context Lean (0-1 seconds)
step.action: Establish topic quickly in 5-8 words
step.purpose: Gives viewer just enough context to understand what you're discussing

step.title: Scroll Stop Interjection (1-3 seconds)
step.action: Insert surprise element with interjection like "But get this..." or "Here's the crazy part..."
step.example: "But get this... the screen is actually the least impressive part"
"Here's what nobody tells you... the AI is doing it backwards"
step.purpose: Pattern interrupt; creates micro-suspense that prevents scroll

step.title: Contrarian Snapback (3-5 seconds)
step.action: Deliver unexpected insight that contradicts common belief
step.example: "The real innovation is the cooling system they built underneath"
"The best results come from using LESS prompting, not more"
step.purpose: Pays off the curiosity gap; rewards viewer for staying engaged

key_metrics.metric: Measures if Context Lean + Interjection successfully stopped scroll
Validates that Contrarian Snapback delivered on promise (not clickbait)

failure_mode.name: Clickbait Trap
failure_mode.symptoms: High Hook Rate (>40%) but low Hold Rate (<10%); low conversion rate
failure_mode.root_cause: Interjection creates expectation that Snapback doesn't fulfill
failure_mode.diagnosis: Watch video: Does the Snapback actually deliver surprising insight or is it generic?
failure_mode.recovery: Rewrite Snapback to include concrete, specific, contrarian detail
failure_mode.prevention: Test Snapback on 3 people: "Is this insight actually surprising or expected?"

golden_run.scenario: SaaS product explaining AI workflow automation; need hook that stands out in crowded market

inputs.target_audience: Busy entrepreneurs drowning in admin work

execution.step_1: Context Lean: "AI can automate your admin work"
execution.step_3: Snapback: "...most people automate the WRONG tasks first and waste 10 hours setting it up"
execution.continuation: Video continues explaining the RIGHT tasks to automate first

expected_outputs.output: Comments: "Wait, which tasks should I automate first?" (engagement spike)

success_criteria.criterion: Hold Rate >15% (Snapback delivered value, not clickbait)
Engagement rate >2% (comments asking for more detail)

framework.name: Lazy Static Headlines
framework.key_quote: "Choose a pain point that your audience is struggling with and add the word gone at the end."
framework.summary: Eleven text-overlay formulas for static images that require zero copywriting skill. Designed for high-volume static testing where creative production speed is critical. Each formula follows a proven pattern that clearly communicates benefit or transformation.

when_to_use.trigger: High-volume static testing (need 10+ variants quickly)
When copywriting resources are limited
Direct response ecom or supplement offers
Pain-point driven products (health, beauty, productivity)

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Luxury/aspirational brands (too direct, not sophisticated enough)
Complex products requiring education (static can't explain mechanism)

formula.name: Timeline Ad
formula.example: "24 Hours: Pain Relief | 3 Days: Blood Flow | 3 Weeks: Permanent Fix"
formula.purpose: Increases AOV by encouraging bulk purchases for long-term results

formula.name: 3 Reasons Why
formula.example: "3 Reasons Why This Serum Reverses Wrinkles"

formula.name: Before I Discovered
formula.pattern: Before I Discovered [Product], I Struggled With [Pain]
formula.example: "Before I Discovered Collagen Peptides, I Struggled With Joint Pain"

formula.name: The Problem With
formula.example: "The Problem With Most Probiotics Is They Don't Survive Your Stomach Acid"

formula.name: How To
formula.example: "How To Lose Weight Without Starving Yourself"

formula.name: If You're Still
formula.example: "If You're Still Waking Up Tired, Try This"

formula.name: Stop Doing
formula.example: "Stop Using Hot Water. It's Causing Your Dry Skin"

formula.name: The Real Reason
formula.example: "The Real Reason You Can't Sleep (It's Not Caffeine)"

production_workflow.step: Select 1 high-quality product image or lifestyle photo
Apply all 11 formulas as text overlays
Launch in 3:2:2 or Pack System for testing

key_metrics.metric: Static text-heavy ads typically lower CTR than video; >1.5% is strong

metric.name: CPA Comparison
metric.threshold: Best formula should be 30%+ better than median

key_metrics.metric: Identifies which pain point/formula resonates most

golden_run.scenario: Ecom supplement brand needs 10 static ad variants for weekly testing

inputs.pain_points: Knee pain, joint stiffness, mobility issues

execution.action: Step 1: Use existing product photo (bottle on white background)
execution.variant_4: "3 Reasons This Works For Knee Pain"
execution.variant_6: "The Problem With Joint Supplements Is They Don't Absorb"
execution.variant_7: "How To Fix Knee Pain Without Surgery"
execution.variant_9: "Stop Ignoring Knee Pain. It's Getting Worse"
execution.variant_10: "The Real Reason Your Joints Hurt (It's Not Age)"
execution.action: Step 4: Upload all to Prospecting CBO as Pack

expected_outputs.output: Total production time: 90 minutes for 11 variants
Day 7 results: "Knee Pain Gone" and "Timeline" variants emerge as winners (40% spend share)
Losers: "3 Reasons Why" gets <5% spend (too vague)
Learning: Direct pain-point framing outperforms indirect education

success_criteria.criterion: At least 2 variants achieve CPA <target by Day 7
Clear winner emerges (30%+ better CPA than median) for scaling

framework.name: Founder-Led VSL
framework.key_quote: "Face-to-camera storytelling from the business owner building authority."
framework.summary: Authentic video from founder explaining why they started the business, what problem they saw in the industry, and how their unique solution works. Builds trust through vulnerability and authority.

when_to_use.trigger: Building trust for new brands or high-ticket offers
Competitive markets where differentiation is critical
Complex products needing founder expertise to explain

step.title: Personal Narrative
step.action: Share why you started this business (the origin story)

step.title: Agitate Industry Problem
step.action: Explain what's broken in your industry and why competitors miss the mark

step.title: Unique Mechanism
step.action: Reveal your specific solution and why it works when others don't

step.title: Risk Reversal
step.action: Offer guarantee or proof (money-back, testimonials, results)

key_metrics.metric: Founder videos should retain attention longer than UGC
Viewers google brand name after seeing founder

framework.name: Scam Hook
framework.key_quote: "I thought this was a SCAM... because the price was too low."
framework.summary: Leverages loss aversion by suggesting product might be scam, then pivoting to validity proof. Creates curiosity gap that compels engagement from skeptical audiences.

when_to_use.trigger: Skeptical audiences (high-ticket offers, supplements, info products)
Products with "too good to be true" positioning

step.title: Scam Accusation Hook
step.action: "I thought this was a SCAM..."

step.title: Reason Pivot
step.action: "...because [price too low / results too good / testimonials unbelievable]"

step.title: Validation
step.action: Show proof: clinical studies, testimonials, money-back guarantee

framework.name: Nano Banana AI Workflow
framework.summary: Using Gemini (Nano Banana Pro model) to iterate winning static ads by swapping elements (model, background, product) while keeping composition identical. Enables rapid scaling of winning concepts.

when_to_use.trigger: Scaling a winning static image (proven CPA, high spend)
Need 10+ variations quickly to combat fatigue

step.title: Upload Winner
step.action: Upload winning ad image to Gemini

step.title: Prompt for Swap
step.action: "Replace [Element A] with [Element B], keep composition identical"

step.title: Generate Variations
step.action: Create 5-10 variants (different models, backgrounds, times of day)

step.title: Launch Pack
step.action: Upload all to new Ad Set for testing

framework.name: Hero vs. Villain Script
framework.summary: Identifying underlying pain points (Villain) from customer reviews and positioning product as specific Hero. Creates clear narrative arc and emotional resonance.

when_to_use.trigger: Competitive markets where differentiation is needed
Products with clear "before/after" transformation

step.title: Research
step.action: Find deep pain points in bad Amazon reviews of competitors

step.title: Name the Villain
step.action: Call out the specific struggle (e.g., "Confidence on a first date")

step.title: Introduce Hero
step.action: Position product as the only solution to that specific Villain

framework.name: Indirect vs. Direct Image Spectrum
framework.summary: Using "Indirect" ads (curiosity-driven, no product shown) for cold traffic scale vs "Direct" ads (product + offer) for retargeting. Matches creative to awareness level.

when_to_use.trigger: Cold traffic scaling (Prospecting campaigns)
Retargeting warmed audiences

pattern.indirect: Image creates curiosity, copy sells the click. Example: "She's 57, here's why she has zero belly fat" (no product visible)
pattern.direct: Product image + "Buy Now" + Discount. Used for retargeting audiences already aware of product

framework.name: Give Me Time Hook
framework.summary: Asking for upfront time commitment to filter for high-intent viewers. Creates psychological contract and increases hold rate.

when_to_use.trigger: Educational or long-form content (60+ seconds)
High-ticket offers where lead quality matters more than volume

framework.script_pattern: "Give me 30 seconds and I'll show you how to fix [Problem]"
framework.visual: Show countdown timer or fast-paced editing to create urgency

framework.name: Visual Hook Architecture
framework.summary: Controlling the eye path in the first 3 seconds through visual composition. Prevents scroll by creating single focal point and using satisfying/gross textures.

when_to_use.trigger: All video creative
When testing new visual concepts

step.title: Single Focal Point
step.action: Remove visual chaos; eye should go to ONE element (product, face, weird texture)

step.title: Texture Curiosity
step.action: Use "satisfying" (slime, foam, peeling) or "gross" (weird rocks, unusual food) textures

step.title: Native Familiarity
step.action: Establish brand early but keep format native (UGC-style, not commercial)

framework.name: Post ID Harvesting
framework.summary: Moving winning ad from test to scale environment by copying Post ID to preserve social proof. See Campaign Architecture module for full framework detail.
framework.integration_note: Full framework detailed in KB-CAMPAIGN-ARCHITECTURE-v1.0 Framework F4. Reference that module for complete implementation steps.

framework.name: Concept Over Variation
framework.principle: Meta's Andromeda algorithm penalizes repetitive formats and rewards concept diversity. Testing must involve distinct concepts (different hooks/stories/mechanisms), not minor variations (color swaps, button positions).

validation_checklist.item: Do creatives have different hooks? (Not same hook, different color)
Do creatives tell different stories? (Founder journey vs customer testimonial vs product demo)
Do creatives use different proof types? (Authority vs social proof vs scientific data)

framework.rule: IF 80% of creative elements are the same across variants → You're testing variations, not concepts

framework.name: Creative Flywheel (Rule of 1:10k)
framework.production_rule: For every $10,000 in monthly ad spend, produce 1 new creative concept per week

weekly_routine.step: Analyze last 7-14 days using Incremental Attribution
Source 1 new concept from competitors (Meta Ads Library, MagicBrief)
Launch new batch as dated "Pack" in Prospecting CBO

category.name: Diagnostic
category.heuristic: IF Hook Rate is <25% THEN audit the Visual Hook (first 3s) for "Visual Chaos" or lack of focal point [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 811]
IF Hook Rate is >35% but Hold Rate is <15% THEN you have a "Clickbait" issue; the video body does not deliver on the hook's promise [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 424]
IF CPMs spike >50% week-over-week THEN your creative diversity is low; launch a new "Concept" immediately [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 372]
IF CTR is high (>2%) but Conversion Rate is low THEN the ad-to-lander congruency is broken; check the "Post-Click" experience [SRC: blake ecom | 415]

category.name: Optimization
category.heuristic: IF a static ad performs well THEN use AI (Nano Banana) to generate 10 variations of model/background immediately [SRC: Sam Piliero | 617]
IF you are under $1M revenue THEN focus on ONE avatar with multiple angles, rather than multiple avatars [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 54]
IF scaling a winner THEN do not turn off the original; create a new "Pack" to run alongside it [SRC: Sam Piliero | 509]

category.name: Scaling
category.heuristic: IF spending $10k/month THEN produce at least 1 new ad per week (Rule of 1:10k) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]
IF an ad has high "Incremental Attribution" THEN it is a true scaler; graduate it to the scaling campaign [SRC: Sam Piliero | 191]

category.name: Troubleshooting
category.heuristic: IF viewers drop off after the hook THEN insert a "Scroll Stop Interjection" (e.g., "But get this...") to re-engage [SRC: Kallaway | 352]
IF static ads are failing THEN test "Native" formats (Apple Notes, Twitter screenshots, News Articles) [SRC: Omar Eddaoudi | 268]

sop.name: Weekly Creative Flywheel
sop.summary: Analyze winning concepts → Iterate winners → Source 1 new concept from competitors → Launch as Pack

sop.name: Concept Diversity Validation (Pre-Launch)

steps.step: Review all creatives in test batch side-by-side
Check: Do they have different hooks? (Not same hook, different text overlay color)
Check: Do they tell different stories? (Founder vs customer vs demo = different stories)
Check: Do they use different proof types? (Authority vs social vs scientific)
IF >80% similar → REJECT; create new concepts, not variations

sop.quality_gate: Minimum 3 meaningfully different concepts per test batch

sop.name: Nano Banana Production Sprint
sop.summary: Identify winning static → Upload to Gemini → Generate 10 variants → Launch as Pack

failure_mode.name: Creative Fatigue (Andromeda)
failure_mode.symptoms: Rising CPA, dropping CTR on previously winning ads
failure_mode.root_cause: Audience saturation of a specific format; algorithm penalizes repetition
failure_mode.recovery: Launch a new "Concept" (not variation) in a fresh pack

failure_mode.name: Hook-Body Mismatch
failure_mode.symptoms: High Hook Rate (>40%), low Hold Rate, low ROAS
failure_mode.root_cause: Clickbait hook sets expectations the product/video cannot fulfill
failure_mode.recovery: Re-edit the video body to match the hook's intensity or tone
failure_mode.prevention: Test script with 3 people: "Does the body deliver on what the hook promises?"

failure_mode.name: The 'Polished' Trap
failure_mode.symptoms: High production value ads getting low CTR
failure_mode.root_cause: Ads look like ads; users scroll past commercial content
failure_mode.recovery: Switch to "Ugly" ads, native formats, or founder-shot content
failure_mode.prevention: Native format audit pre-launch: "Does this look like a friend's post or an ad?"

failure_mode.name: Variation Testing Loop
failure_mode.symptoms: Testing 50 ads but performance remains flat
failure_mode.root_cause: Testing button colors or small text tweaks instead of distinct Concepts
failure_mode.recovery: Adopt the "Concept vs. Variation" protocol; create 5 entirely new concepts
failure_mode.prevention: Concept Diversity Validation SOP before every launch

knowledge_block.metrics_table: <metric name="Hook Rate (3s Video Plays / Impressions)" purpose="Attention capture" threshold=">30%" action_trigger="IF <25% → Audit first 3 seconds visual hook" source="[SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 849]"/>

patterns.pattern: For every $10,000 in monthly spend, produce 1 new creative concept per week. $100k/month = 10 new concepts/week.
Test different stories/hooks/mechanisms, not button colors or font changes. 3 concepts beats 30 variations.
"Ugly" ads, Notes App format, News Article style outperform polished commercial content by bypassing ad blindness.
Harvesting Post IDs (preserving likes/comments) reduces CPM by 10-30% vs duplicate ads.
First 3 seconds determine 80% of performance. Optimize hook before body.

checklist.name: Pre-Launch Creative Validation
checklist.item: [ ] Concept Diversity Check: Are these 3+ meaningfully different concepts? [SRC: Alex Robinson | 121]
[ ] Hook Audit: Does each video have clear focal point in first 3 seconds? [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 849]
[ ] Native Format Check: Do statics look like UGC/organic content, not ads? [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 10]
[ ] Text Overlay Readability: Can text be read in 1 second on mobile? [SRC: Chase Chappell | 730]
[ ] Hook-Body Alignment: Does video body deliver on hook promise? [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 424]
[ ] Production Volume: Meeting Rule of 1:10k requirement? [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]

checklist.name: Weekly Creative Review
checklist.item: [ ] Check Incremental ROAS on all active creatives [SRC: Sam Piliero | 595]
[ ] Identify winning "Concepts" (not just individual ads) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 196]
[ ] Check Frequency (>3.0 = creative fatigue) [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 391]
[ ] Brief 4-6 iterations of winning concept [SRC: Sam Piliero | 196]
[ ] Source 1 new concept from competitor research [SRC: Sam Piliero | 613]
[ ] Harvest winning Post IDs to scaling campaign [SRC: Professor Charley T | 103]

compliance_guardrails.guardrail: Concept Diversity Required: Must test different hooks/stories/mechanisms, not color variations. Andromeda penalizes repetitive formats [SRC: Alex Robinson | 121]
Rule of 1:10k Production Minimum: For every $10k monthly spend, produce 1 new creative concept per week to prevent fatigue [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]
No Fake Urgency or Manipulative Hooks: Hooks must be authentic; no false scarcity, fake countdown timers, or deceptive claims [Constitutional Compliance]
Hook-Body Alignment: Video body must deliver on hook promise to avoid clickbait classification and low Hold Rates [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 424]
Native Format Preference: Prioritize UGC-style, Notes App, or News Article formats over polished commercial content [SRC: Franky Shaw | 235]
First 3 Seconds Critical: Optimize hook before body; 80% of performance determined in first 3 seconds [SRC: Kallaway | 350]
